name: Montana Dental Assistant Registration
description: Complete guide to dental assistant requirements in Montana — fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.

address.description: 301 S Park Ave, 4th Floor, P.O. Box 200513, Helena, MT 59620-0513

offers.name: Renewal Fee
offers.description: N/A - Montana does not license general Dental Assistants

processingTime: N/A - Montana does not license general Dental Assistants
renewalCycle: N/A - Montana does not license general Dental Assistants

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Training
requiredDocuments.description: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

requiredDocuments.name: DANB Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

requiredDocuments.name: Radiography Certification
requiredDocuments.description: Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

mainEntity.name: Does Montana require a dental assistant license?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, Montana does not require state licensure for general dental assistants. However, expanded functions and radiography may require permits or certifications.

mainEntity.name: Is DANB certification recognized in Montana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Montana.

mainEntity.name: Can I take dental x-rays in Montana?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

mainEntity.name: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Montana?

acceptedAnswer.text: There is no state application fee for general dental assistants in Montana. However, DANB CDA exam costs $450, and radiography certifications may have additional fees.
